full_context: Compare the calculated induced voltage with the safety limit specified by code to determine safety.

variable_definitions.name: Calculated Induced Voltage
variable_definitions.meaning: Calculated power-frequency induced voltage at a point on cable metal sheath

variable_definitions.name: Safety Limit
variable_definitions.meaning: Maximum allowable induced voltage per code

potential_questions: Given E_s=90V, Limit=50V, judge if safe
task_description: 1. Obtain calculated induced voltage E_s; 2. Obtain applicable safety limit; 3. Compare E_s and Limit; 4. If E_s ≤ Limit, output 'Safe'; otherwise output 'Unsafe, measures required'.

full_context: Calculate rated output current of a photovoltaic power station using three-phase apparent power formula.

variable_definitions.name: Rated Current
variable_definitions.meaning: Output current at rated condition of PV station

variable_definitions.name: Rated Capacity
variable_definitions.meaning: Rated apparent power of PV station

variable_definitions.name: Rated Voltage
variable_definitions.meaning: Rated line voltage at point of common coupling

task_description: 1. Obtain rated capacity P_N (convert to VA); 2. Obtain rated voltage U_N (convert to V); 3. Calculate I_N = P_N / (√3 × U_N); 4. Output I_N.

full_context: No dynamic reactive current support is required when PCC voltage is above 0.9 times rated voltage.

variable_definitions.name: Voltage Per-Unit Value
variable_definitions.meaning: Ratio of PCC voltage to rated voltage

task_description: 1. Obtain voltage per-unit U_T; 2. Judge if U_T > 0.9; 3. If true, output I_T=0; else calculate by low-voltage ride-through formula.

full_context: Multiply overvoltage calculated at plain altitude by altitude correction factor to get actual overvoltage at high altitude.

variable_definitions.name: High-Altitude Overvoltage
variable_definitions.meaning: Representative overvoltage corrected for altitude

variable_definitions.name: Plain Overvoltage
variable_definitions.meaning: Representative overvoltage calculated for plain condition

variable_definitions.name: Altitude Correction Factor
variable_definitions.meaning: Factor correcting external insulation strength with altitude

task_description: 1. Obtain plain overvoltage U_rp0; 2. Obtain altitude correction factor K_a; 3. Calculate U_rp_H = U_rp0 × K_a; 4. Output result.

full_context: During overcurrent, the additional winding hot-spot temperature rise is approximately proportional to (I²-1) multiplied by time.

variable_definitions.name: Additional Hot-Spot Temperature Rise
variable_definitions.meaning: Extra hot-spot rise during overcurrent

variable_definitions.name: Temperature Rise Coefficient
variable_definitions.meaning: Coefficient related to winding heat capacity and resistance

variable_definitions.name: Overcurrent Ratio
variable_definitions.meaning: Ratio of actual current to rated current

variable_definitions.name: Duration
variable_definitions.meaning: Overcurrent duration

potential_questions: Given α=0.5 K·s, I=1.2, t=30s, calculate Δθ_h_add
task_description: 1. Determine temperature rise coefficient α (given or typical); 2. Obtain overcurrent ratio I; 3. Obtain duration t; 4. Calculate Δθ_h_add = α × (I² - 1) × t; 5. Output result.

full_context: Calculate CO₂ emission per kWh of electricity supplied based on standard coal consumption rate and emission factor.

variable_definitions.name: Carbon Emission Intensity
variable_definitions.meaning: CO₂ emission per kWh of electricity supplied

variable_definitions.name: Standard Coal Consumption Rate
variable_definitions.meaning: Standard coal consumed per kWh

variable_definitions.name: Emission Factor
variable_definitions.meaning: CO₂ produced per kg of standard coal

potential_questions: Given b_gn=320 g/(kW·h), EF=2.66 kg_CO2/kg_coal, calculate CE
task_description: 1. Obtain standard coal consumption rate b_gn; 2. Obtain emission factor EF; 3. Unit conversion: convert b_gn to kg/(kW·h) (divide by 1000); 4. Calculate CE = (b_gn / 1000) × EF; 5. Output result.

full_context: The final selected cross-section of protective conductor shall not be less than the larger of two minimum sections from simplified and detailed formulas.

variable_definitions.name: Simplified Formula Minimum Section
variable_definitions.meaning: Minimum section from simplified formula

variable_definitions.name: Detailed Formula Minimum Section
variable_definitions.meaning: Minimum section from detailed material formula

variable_definitions.name: Selected Section
variable_definitions.meaning: Actual selected conductor cross-section

potential_questions: Given S_min_simple=120mm², S_min_detail=115mm², S_selected=120mm², verify compliance
task_description: 1. Obtain simplified result S_min_simple; 2. Obtain detailed result S_min_detail; 3. Obtain selected section S_selected; 4. Verify S_selected ≥ max(S_min_simple, S_min_detail); 5. Output verification result.

full_context: Calculate exponential decay term in cooling curve.

variable_definitions.name: Exponential Decay Factor
variable_definitions.meaning: Coefficient decaying exponentially with time

variable_definitions.name: Time
variable_definitions.meaning: Time after power-off

variable_definitions.name: Time Constant
variable_definitions.meaning: Characteristic time of decay speed

task_description: 1. Obtain time t; 2. Obtain time constant T; 3. Calculate exponent -t/T; 4. Calculate e^{-t/T}; 5. Output f(t).

full_context: At t=0, exponential term e^0=1 and linear term=0, so average winding temperature at power-off is sum of A_0 and g.

variable_definitions.name: Winding Temperature at Power-Off
variable_definitions.meaning: Average winding temperature at moment of power-off (t=0)

variable_definitions.name: Constant Term
variable_definitions.meaning: Initial estimated liquid average temperature

variable_definitions.name: Temperature Gradient
variable_definitions.meaning: Winding-to-oil temperature difference at power-off

task_description: 1. Obtain parameter A_0; 2. Obtain parameter g; 3. Calculate θ_w0 = A_0 + g; 4. Output result.

full_context: Calculate flicker limit allocated to a specific user based on total PCC limit, capacity ratio, and simultaneity factor.

variable_definitions.name: User 2 Flicker Limit
variable_definitions.meaning: Allowable long-term flicker from User 2

variable_definitions.name: Total PCC Limit
variable_definitions.meaning: Total allowable flicker at PCC

variable_definitions.name: User 2 Capacity
variable_definitions.meaning: Contracted capacity of User 2

variable_definitions.name: Total Supply Capacity
variable_definitions.meaning: Total supply capacity at PCC

variable_definitions.name: Simultaneity Factor
variable_definitions.meaning: Simultaneity rate of fluctuating loads

potential_questions: Given G=0.5, S_2=10MVA, S_t=50MVA, F=0.25, calculate E_2
task_description: 1. Obtain total PCC limit G; 2. Obtain User 2 capacity S_2; 3. Obtain total capacity S_t; 4. Obtain simultaneity factor F; 5. Calculate cube root ∛(S_2/S_t); 6. Calculate E_2 = G × cube_root / F; 7. Output E_2.

full_context: Calculate sum of cubes of short-term flicker values for long-term flicker statistics.

variable_definitions.name: Sum of Cubes
variable_definitions.meaning: Total sum of cubes of all short-term flicker values

potential_questions: Given 12 P_stj values, calculate their sum of cubes
task_description: 1. Obtain array of short-term flicker values P_stj; 2. Compute cube (P_stj^3) for each value; 3. Sum all cube values; 4. Output Sum_Cube.

full_context: Compare calculated long-term flicker value with specified limit to judge if exceeded.

variable_definitions.name: Long-Term Flicker Value
variable_definitions.meaning: Statistical long-term flicker level

variable_definitions.name: Flicker Limit
variable_definitions.meaning: Allowable limit specified by standard

potential_questions: Given P_lt=0.95, L=1.0, judge if exceeded
task_description: 1. Obtain long-term flicker value P_lt; 2. Obtain flicker limit L; 3. Compare P_lt and L; 4. If P_lt > L, output 'Exceeded'; else output 'Qualified'.

full_context: Add frequency correction to reference frequency interference field strength to get field strength at target frequency.

variable_definitions.name: Corrected Field Strength
variable_definitions.meaning: Radio interference field strength at target frequency

variable_definitions.name: Reference Field Strength
variable_definitions.meaning: Interference field strength at reference frequency (0.5MHz)

variable_definitions.name: Frequency Correction
variable_definitions.meaning: Field strength change due to frequency variation

potential_questions: Given E=58 dB(μV/m), ΔE=-1.5 dB, calculate E1
task_description: 1. Obtain reference field strength E; 2. Obtain frequency correction ΔE; 3. Calculate E1 = E + ΔE; 4. Output E1.

full_context: Estimate potential rise caused by lightning current through impulse grounding resistance, which affects line insulation coordination.

variable_definitions.name: Impulse Potential Rise
variable_definitions.meaning: Peak potential on grounding device from lightning current

variable_definitions.name: Lightning Current Amplitude
variable_definitions.meaning: Peak lightning current through tower grounding

variable_definitions.name: Impulse Grounding Resistance
variable_definitions.meaning: Equivalent grounding resistance under lightning current

task_description: 1. Estimate or obtain lightning current I_impulse (typical or given); 2. Obtain impulse grounding resistance R_i; 3. Calculate U_rise = I_impulse × R_i; 4. Output U_rise.

full_context: Height of conductor at mid-span sag point equals average height minus maximum sag approximately.

variable_definitions.name: Height at Sag Point
variable_definitions.meaning: Height of conductor at lowest sag point above ground

variable_definitions.name: Average Height
variable_definitions.meaning: Average height of conductor suspension points

variable_definitions.name: Maximum Sag
variable_definitions.meaning: Conductor droop at mid-span

task_description: 1. Obtain average conductor height H_avg; 2. Obtain maximum sag f; 3. Calculate H_sag = H_avg - f; 4. Output H_sag.

full_context: Verify if ground clearance at maximum sag meets minimum safety distance requirement.

variable_definitions.name: Height at Sag Point
variable_definitions.meaning: Height of conductor lowest point above ground

variable_definitions.name: Minimum Ground Clearance
variable_definitions.meaning: Minimum safety distance required by code

potential_questions: Given H_sag=17m, D_min=7.5m, judge if satisfied
task_description: 1. Obtain height at sag H_sag; 2. Obtain minimum ground clearance D_min; 3. Compare H_sag and D_min; 4. If H_sag ≥ D_min, output 'Satisfied'; else output 'Not Satisfied'.

full_context: Calculate moment of inertia of rectangular section about its neutral axis (through centroid, parallel to base).

variable_definitions.name: Moment of Inertia
variable_definitions.meaning: Moment of inertia of rectangular section about neutral axis

variable_definitions.name: Width
variable_definitions.meaning: Width of rectangular section

variable_definitions.name: Height
variable_definitions.meaning: Height of rectangular section

task_description: 1. Obtain width b and height h (convert to meters); 2. Calculate h³; 3. Calculate I_z = (b * h³) / 12; 4. Output I_z.

full_context: Calculate design standard coal consumption rate for power generation based on design thermal efficiency.

variable_definitions.name: Design Standard Coal Consumption Rate
variable_definitions.meaning: Standard coal consumed per unit electricity generation

variable_definitions.name: Design Thermal Efficiency
variable_definitions.meaning: Efficiency of converting fuel heat to electricity

task_description: 1. Obtain design thermal efficiency η_fn; 2. Calculate b_fn = 0.123 / η_fn × 100000; 3. Output b_fn.

full_context: Formula for capacitive reactance of a capacitor in AC circuit.

variable_definitions.name: Capacitive Reactance
variable_definitions.meaning: Opposition of capacitor to AC current

variable_definitions.name: Frequency
variable_definitions.meaning: AC frequency

variable_definitions.name: Capacitance
variable_definitions.meaning: Capacitance of capacitor

task_description: 1. Obtain frequency f and capacitance C; 2. Calculate 2πfC; 3. Calculate X_C = 1/(2πfC); 4. Output X_C.

full_context: Formula for reactive power supplied by a capacitor in AC circuit.

variable_definitions.name: Reactive Power
variable_definitions.meaning: Power exchanged between capacitor and source

variable_definitions.name: Capacitor Current
variable_definitions.meaning: RMS current through capacitor

variable_definitions.name: Capacitive Reactance
variable_definitions.meaning: Capacitive reactance of capacitor

task_description: 1. Obtain current I_C and reactance X_C; 2. Calculate I_C²; 3. Calculate Q_C = I_C² × X_C; 4. Output Q_C.

full_context: In mechanical design, allowable stress of material is usually based on yield strength and safety factor.

variable_definitions.name: Allowable Stress
variable_definitions.meaning: Maximum stress allowed for design

variable_definitions.name: Yield Strength
variable_definitions.meaning: Stress at which material begins obvious plastic deformation

variable_definitions.name: Safety Factor
variable_definitions.meaning: Strength reserve factor for safety

task_description: 1. Obtain material yield strength σ_y; 2. Obtain safety factor n; 3. Calculate [σ] = σ_y / n; 4. Output [σ].

full_context: Calculate cross-sectional area of a circular pipe.

variable_definitions.name: Cross-Sectional Area
variable_definitions.meaning: Internal flow area of pipe

variable_definitions.name: Pipe Inner Diameter
variable_definitions.meaning: Inside diameter of pipe

task_description: 1. Obtain pipe inner diameter d; 2. Calculate radius r=d/2; 3. Calculate area A=π×r²; 4. Output A.

full_context: Average flow velocity equals volumetric flow rate divided by cross-sectional area.

variable_definitions.name: Average Flow Velocity
variable_definitions.meaning: Average fluid speed over pipe cross-section

variable_definitions.name: Volumetric Flow Rate
variable_definitions.meaning: Fluid volume per unit time through section

variable_definitions.name: Cross-Sectional Area
variable_definitions.meaning: Area fluid passes through

potential_questions: Given Q=0.02 m³/s, A=0.00785 m², calculate v
task_description: 1. Obtain flow rate Q and area A; 2. Calculate v = Q / A; 3. Output v.

full_context: Calculate compressive stress on a uniformly compressed member section.

variable_definitions.name: Compressive Stress
variable_definitions.meaning: Pressure per unit area

variable_definitions.name: Axial Compressive Force
variable_definitions.meaning: Axial force acting on member

variable_definitions.name: Cross-Sectional Area
variable_definitions.meaning: Load-bearing area of member

potential_questions: Given N=1.5e6 N, A=0.1 m², calculate σ
task_description: 1. Obtain axial force N (convert to N) and area A; 2. Calculate σ = N / A; 3. Ensure unit consistency (convert Pa to MPa); 4. Output σ.
